{"step_by_step_thinking": "The masseter muscle originates on the zygomatic arch and maxilla and inserts on the posterolateral aspect of the mandibular angle. The buccinator muscle is a facial muscle that lies deep to the skin of the cheek and is not associated with the zygomatic process of the maxilla. The middle temporal muscle originates at the temporal fossa and is inserted on the medial aspect of the mandible's ramus and coronoid process. The medial pterygoid muscle originates from the maxillary tuberosity and the lateral pterygoid plate and inserts on the medial surface of the mandibular angle and ramus. Among these options, the masseter is the only muscle that attaches to the zygomatic process of the maxilla.", "answer_choice": "A"}
